g.text: All these services communicate with nginx by first exposing their own port, so

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: they can be accessed through the network interfaces (eno1, tun0); the connections

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: are not shown to keep the diagram clean.

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: The setup on the right of the black magic circle (this being

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: the many routers that your ISP uses to route packets to

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: and from the server) is only needed because of my not

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji
Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: If I did have a static IP, the setup shown to its left will

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: be sufficient, and global clients will effectively be treated

text.font-family: Excalifont, Xiaolai, sans-serif, Segoe UI Emoji

g.text: as local clients insofar as they can access exposed ports
